text.title: Django Import Convention Rules
text.summary: Rules for import ordering, cross-app aliasing, and circular-import prevention in Django multi-app projects.

rule.statement: Import cross-app modules via their module alias: from apps.orders import models as order_models. Never import symbols directly: from apps.orders.models import Order.
rule.rationale: Django projects with many apps accumulate model name collisions: User, Order, Item appear in multiple apps. Aliases make the source app explicit at every call site and enable reliable automated refactoring.

rule.statement: Use a consistent alias suffix per module type: _models, _services, _constants. Never mix order_models and orders_models in the same project.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent alias names cause the same name-collision problem the convention exists to solve. Pin the pattern in pyproject.toml documentation and enforce via AST linter.

rule.statement: Order imports in four tiers: (1) __future__, (2) stdlib, (3) third-party (django, rest_framework, celery), (4) cross-app aliased, (5) relative from . import.
rule.rationale: PEP 8 / isort canonical order. Consistent ordering eliminates diff noise and makes it trivial to see which tier an import belongs to at a glance.

rule.statement: Never use wildcard imports: from apps.orders.models import * is unconditionally banned.
rule.rationale: Wildcards make it impossible to determine which names are in scope, break tools like mypy and pylint, and re-create the name-collision problem the alias rule solves.

rule.statement: Gate type-only cross-app imports behind if TYPE_CHECKING: and add from __future__ import annotations at the file top.
rule.rationale: Cross-app imports between models.py files are the primary source of Django circular imports. TYPE_CHECKING is False at runtime, so the import never executes. __future__ annotations defers annotation evaluation, making the type hint work without the runtime import.

rule.statement: DRF serializer Meta.model requires a runtime symbol; do not gate it under TYPE_CHECKING. Use apps.get_model("app_label", "ModelName") or a direct runtime import.
rule.rationale: Serializers introspect Meta.model at class creation time. A TYPE_CHECKING-gated import makes the symbol undefined at runtime, causing an AttributeError on serializer load.

rule.statement: Exclude migrations/ from the alias linter. Django's migration loader parses model references as strings; agent-generated alias rewrites in migrations break the loader.
rule.rationale: Migration files use "users.User" string references that are resolved by Django's app registry, not Python imports. Rewriting them to use_models.User breaks makemigrations and migrate.
